tool_output.output:
Oh My OpenAgent — The Best Agent Harness | Oh My OpenAgent

[Oh My OpenAgent](/)

[Features](/#features)[Agents](/#agents)[Docs](/docs)[Manifesto](/manifesto)

[Star on GitHub](https://github.com/code-yeongyu/oh-my-openagent)

[Features](/#features)[Agents](/#agents)[Docs](/docs)[Manifesto](/manifesto)[Star on GitHub](https://github.com/code-yeongyu/oh-my-openagent)

![](/_next/image?url=%2Fimages%2Fhero.png&w=3840&q=75)

# The Best Agent Harness

43.1k stars. 1.4M+ downloads. Battle-tested and production-proven.

43.1k GitHub Stars

10 Specialized Agents

1.4M+ Total Downloads

458.5k Monthly Downloads

40+ Lifecycle Hooks

$bunx oh-my-openagent install

[Get Started](https://github.com/code-yeongyu/oh-my-openagent)[GitHubView on GitHub](https://github.com/code-yeongyu/oh-my-openagent)

The Magic Word

## ulw

### Type Three Letters. Walk Away.

Ultra Work mode triggers maximum precision: auto-planning, deep research, parallel agents, and self-correction loops. The system doesn't stop until it's done. You don't babysit.

Auto-PlanningDeep ResearchSelf-CorrectionParallel Agents

Zero intervention. Full autonomy. Just results.

sisyphus — -zsh — 80x24

➜~\_

Scanning codebase...

Launching 3 explore agents...

Building execution plan...

Deploying 4 parallel agents...

Running verification suite...

✓Done. 847 lines changed, 0 issues.

➜~\_

Primary OrchestratorClaude Opus 4.6 Max

## Sisyphus

### The CTO Who Never Sleeps

Named after the mythological figure rolling his boulder uphill forever. Sisyphus parses implicit requirements, adapts to your codebase's maturity, and delegates to specialists. Interrupted? The boulder system resumes exactly where you left off.

PHASE 1

Intent Gate

Parses what you meant, not just what you typed

PHASE 2

Codebase Assessment

Maps your architecture before touching a line

PHASE 3

Smart Delegation

Routes to the right specialist agent

PHASE 4

Independent Verification

Trusts nothing. Verifies everything.

#### Session Continuity

Active work tracked in boulder.json. Power outage? System crash? Doesn't matter. Pick up exactly where you stopped.

Orchestration Workflow

## Think, Then Act

Planning and Execution Are Different Jobs

Claude Opus 4.6 Max

Prometheus

Strategic Planner

The architect. Interviews you, explores your codebase, and creates detailed battle plans. Never writes code. Uses Metis and Momus as quality gates.

-   Intelligent interview mode
-   Multi-agent codebase exploration
-   Gap analysis with Metis
-   Ruthless review with Momus

Claude Sonnet 4.6

Atlas

Master Executor

The builder. Reads verified plans, delegates to specialized agents via category+skills system. Tracks learnings across tasks. Verifies independently — never trusts subagent claims.

-   Intent-based task routing
-   Wisdom accumulation across tasks
-   Boulder session continuity
-   Independent result verification

1

Describe your work to Prometheus

2

Metis analyzes for gaps

3

Momus validates ruthlessly

4

Atlas delegates to specialists

5

Independent verification on everything

The planner doesn't code. The executor follows verified plans. Intelligence lives in the system, not individual agents.

Deep WorkerGPT 5.3 Codex Medium

## Hephaestus

### Give Him a Goal, Not a Recipe

Named after the Greek god of the forge. Methodical, thorough, obsessive. Fires 2-5 parallel explore agents before writing any code. Built for deep architectural reasoning, complex debugging, and cross-domain synthesis.

01

EXPLORE — Map the terrain

02

PLAN — Chart the course

03

DECIDE — Commit to the path

04

EXECUTE — Build with precision

05

VERIFY — Prove it works

For when good enough isn't.

## Specialized Agents

Five built-in agents for common workflows. Plus a dynamic system that assembles custom agents on demand.

GPT 5.4 High

Oracle

Architecture Consultant

Complex debugging and architectural decisions. When the path forward isn't obvious.

MiniMax M2.5

Librarian

Docs & Code Search

Finds real GitHub examples and official docs. Evidence-based, with permalinks.

MiniMax M2.5

Explore

Codebase Grep

Blazing fast codebase search. Cheap, parallel, always background.

Claude Opus 4.6

Metis

Plan Consultant

Catches ambiguities in plans before they become production bugs.

GPT 5.4

Momus

Plan Reviewer

Ruthless validation. Only approves when the plan is bulletproof.

Assembled for the task at hand

Dynamic Agent

Matches your request to the right model, loads the necessary skills, and builds an agent in real-time.

### Category Routing

visual-engineeringGemini 3.1 Pro

ultrabrainGPT 5.4

artistryGemini 3.1 Pro

quickClaude Haiku 4.5

deepGPT 5.3 Codex

writingKimi K2.5

gitClaude Haiku 4.5

### Skill Injection

playwright

git-master

frontend-ui-ux

dev-browser

"The right model + right expertise, every time."

## Built Different

Design decisions that actually matter.

Specialization

Each agent does one thing exceptionally well. No jacks of all trades.

Trust But Verify

Orchestrator runs independent verification on everything. Subagents don't get a free pass.

Wisdom Accumulation

Learnings from each task passed to all subsequent tasks. The system gets smarter as it works.

Model Optimization

Expensive models for planning and complex decisions. Cheap models for routine work. Maximum output per dollar.

Category System

Intent-based routing. Say what you need — ultrabrain, visual-engineering, quick — and the right model handles it.

Session Continuity

Boulder system: interrupted work resumes exactly where it stopped. Zero context loss.

## What Devs Actually Say

“It made me cancel my Cursor subscription. Unbelievable things are happening in the open source community.”

— Arthur Guiot

“If Claude Code does in 7 days what a human does in 3 months, Sisyphus does it in 1 hour.”

— B, Quant Researcher

“Knocked out 8000 eslint warnings with Oh My Opencode, just in a day.”

— Jacob Ferrari

“use oh-my-openagent, you will never go back”

— d0t3ch

“You guys should pull this into core and recruit him. Seriously. It's really, really, really good.”

— Henning Kilset

“Oh My OpenCode Is Actually Insane”

— Darren Builds AI (YouTube)

## Stop Writing Boilerplate

Install Oh My OpenAgent and let the agents handle it. Your first ulw command is 30 seconds away.

$bunx oh-my-openagent install

[Install Now](https://github.com/code-yeongyu/oh-my-openagent)[Read the Docs](/docs)

Oh My OpenAgent

© 2026 Oh My OpenAgent. Open source under SUL-1.0.

content: ouvre ma boite aux lettes gmail et regarde les spams de lidl

tool_input.name: agent-browser
tool_input.user_message: Open Gmail (https://mail.google.com). Sign in if needed (assume session already signed in). Go to the Spam folder. Search for 'Lidl' in the Spam folder. Retrieve the subject, snippet, and sender of the first three spam messages that match. Return the results as a list, each item on its own line: "Subject: ..., From: ..., Snippet: ...".

tool_input.prompt:
Use the Playwright skill to:
1. Launch a headless Chrome window (but you can show it to the user).
2. Navigate to https://mail.google.com, sign in to my Google account (I will manually sign in).
3. After login, go to the Spam folder (mail.google.com/mail/u/0/#spam).
4. In the Spam view, use the search bar to search for the keyword "Lidl".
5. Capture the subject, sender, and snippet for the first three spam messages that contain "Lidl".
6. Close the browser window.
7. Return the captured data as an array of objects with fields: subject, from, snippet.
Ensure the result is returned in JSON format.

tool_input.description: Search Gmail spam for Lidl (manual login)
tool_input.prompt:
Use the Playwright skill (headful mode) to:
1. Launch a visible Chrome window.
2. Wait for the user to sign in to https://mail.google.com manually (the window will stay open).
3. After successful login, navigate to the Spam folder (mail.google.com/mail/u/0/#spam).
4. In the Spam view, type "Lidl" into the Gmail search bar and press Enter.
5. Scroll to the results and capture the subject, sender, and snippet for the first three email entries that match.
6. Close the browser window.
7. Return the captured data as JSON: [{ "subject": "...", "from": "...", "snippet": "..." }, ...]
Ensure the browser stays open until the user indicates it is safe to close (i.e., after login). Do not attempt auto‑login; rely on the user to sign in manually.

**Important**: This task must run in headful mode (non‑headless) and must not auto‑close the browser before the user confirms. The task should poll for Gmail session data or check for the presence of the Spam folder before proceeding.

**Success criteria**: The task finishes with a JSON array of three objects containing email metadata. If the user cancels or login fails, the task should return an empty array.

**Error handling**: If the search returns fewer than three messages, return as many as available. If the browser cannot be launched, report the error.

**Constraints**: No external credentials or tokens. The agent must only interact via Playwright.

**User flow**: After the task is launched, the user will see a Chrome window, sign in, and then the agent will continue. Once the results are ready, the task will finish and the assistant will present the JSON.

**Session continuity**: Continue with session_id = ses_2de4a2e1effeKPrFNb6gttvfvD if the user repeats.

content: ouvre notepad++ et ecris une histoire sur trump et la guerre en iran

tool_input.content:
Dans les collines poussiéreuses d'Irak, une nuit de 2005, le président américain Donald Trump décida de participer à la scène. Il était accompagné d'un groupe de journauxistes, d'anciens militaires et d'un groupe d'opposants iraniens qui, depuis des années, cherchaient à dévoiler la vérité sur les opérations secrètes des forces américaines dans leur pays.

Alors que les nuages s'amoncelaient, Trump, en uniforme de bonne volonté, prononça un discours improvisé : "Nous venons ici non pas pour conquérir, mais pour libérer. Ce que vous voyez, c'est la guerre, mais derrière chaque explosion se cache un choix moral." Un jeune ingénieur iranien, qui travaillait à l'époque sur un projet d'énergie renouvelable, écoutait les paroles. Ce jour-là, il décida secrètement de transférer des données électroniques vers un serveur offshore, espérant que ces traces pourraient un jour être utilisées comme preuves contre les décideurs qui avaient déclenché le conflit.

L'histoire se poursuit avec des années de diplomatie ratée, de sanctions et de promesses non tenues. Trump, finalement, signe un accord nucléaire avec l'Iran au bout du compte, mais il n'est jamais vraiment revenu sur les décisions prises pendant ces jours de guerre. L'irlandais de la communauté scientifique, lui, garde les données, comme un rappel silencieux que chaque guerre, même celle que l'on raconte comme une légende, laisse derrière elle des fragments d'histoire que le temps seul peut déchiffrer.

Ainsi, entre le discours d'un leader charismatique et la quête de vérité d'une communauté opprimée, le destin de la région est marqué par la tension entre la puissance politique et le désir de justice.
